pipeline_models._rationale: Cross-model critique. The drafter (Claude Sonnet) and the critic (GPT-5.5) are different models from different vendors. When they agree something is wrong, the signal is much stronger than one model self-critiquing. The decider (Claude Sonnet again) synthesizes the two perspectives. Same pattern gstack uses for /codex review of Claude-authored work.

series_anchor.label: Old Javanese / Kawi tattva corpus (San Hyan series, Vrhaspatitattva, Gaṇapatitattva, Slokantara, Vratisasana, Kunjarakarna, Kalpabuddha)
series_anchor.palette: tropical earth ochre, deep palm-leaf brown, weathered teak, jasmine cream, faint lapis trace
series_anchor.mood: Old Javanese palm-leaf manuscript tradition. Tropical pre-dawn humidity. Frangipani at the threshold. The text inscribed with an iron stylus.
series_anchor.watercolor_treatment: Earth ochre and palm-brown washed wet-on-wet to simulate the patina of palm-leaf age. Tropical greenery suggested only as a faint background tone, never literally rendered. Lapis used as a single bright accent on the subject only if the work demands it.

1_context.content_excerpt:
Homage to the Buddha!

This is the meaning of OṂ AḤ HUṂ. When taken as the foundation for teaching, the Lord of the Three Syllables is the ultimate reality, the *vajra* of body, speech, and mind.

*Come, child, I shall teach you the Great Vehicle, the method of the Path of the Master of Mantras. You are a worthy vessel for the Great Path.*

This Sacred Mahāyāna I shall teach to you. The Sacred *Mantranaya* is called the Mahāyāna, the Great Path. This I shall explain, this I shall teach to you, because you have become a worthy vessel, fit to be taught the sacred doctrine of the *Mantranaya*.

*

output.reasoning: The San Hyan Kamahayanikan opens with the three-syllable vajra teaching — body, speech, and mind as ultimate reality. A single vajra ritual implement, worn and ancient, evokes the transmission of esoteric doctrine without illustrating any scene, and carries the weight of the Kawi tantric corpus naturally.
output.subject: A single bronze vajra ritual scepter, resting at a slight diagonal on a bare surface
output.palette: tropical earth ochre, palm-leaf brown, faint lapis lazuli trace
output.composition: The vajra placed in the lower-left third of the composition, angled slightly rightward, with the upper two-thirds of the warm cream paper left as open breathing room; the object's visual mass anchors the painting without centering it
output.watercolor_treatment: Wet-on-wet earth ochre and palm-brown washes pool darkly along the vajra's central grip and dilute outward to almost nothing at the prongs, simulating aged bronze patina; cold-press paper grain shows through the thinnest washes at the edges; a single restrained touch of diluted lapis at the crown of the upper prongs as the sole accent; faint pencil under-drawing visible beneath the grip; no hard outlines, all edges bleed softly into the cream ground
output.prompt: A single ancient bronze vajra scepter rests at a gentle diagonal in the lower-left third of warm cream cotton paper, its surface rendered in wet-on-wet tropical earth ochre and palm-leaf brown that pool darkest along the central grip and dilute to near-nothing at the outstretched prongs. Cold-press paper grain shows through where pigment thins at the edges, and a faint pencil under-drawing is barely legible beneath the shaft. One restrained touch of diluted lapis lazuli traces the crown of the upper prongs — the sole accent in an otherwise earthen palette. The upper two-thirds of the composition breathes open, unmarked cream. Edges bleed softly into the ground; nothing is hard-lined. The mood is pre-dawn humidity, old palm-leaf, iron stylus on ancient manuscript.

output.reasoning: The critic found no issues and recommends shipping as-is. There are zero issues to evaluate. The original prompt is clean, honors the series anchor fully, and stays within the 80-150 word target. No changes are warranted.
output.final_prompt: A single ancient bronze vajra scepter rests at a gentle diagonal in the lower-left third of warm cream cotton paper, its surface rendered in wet-on-wet tropical earth ochre and palm-leaf brown that pool darkest along the central grip and dilute to near-nothing at the outstretched prongs. Cold-press paper grain shows through where pigment thins at the edges, and a faint pencil under-drawing is barely legible beneath the shaft. One restrained touch of diluted lapis lazuli traces the crown of the upper prongs — the sole accent in an otherwise earthen palette. The upper two-thirds of the composition breathes open, unmarked cream. Edges bleed softly into the ground; nothing is hard-lined. The mood is pre-dawn humidity, old palm-leaf, iron stylus on ancient manuscript.
output.changes_summary: No changes were made. The critic recommended shipping the prompt as-is with no issues identified.
